zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Dean於1622年10月5日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼林肯巴可斯波特迪德哈姆時，她的父親John Dean26歲，母親Susanna Fuller17歲。她於1640年與Thomas Eames在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼迪德哈姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1660年9月17日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可福拉米加姆享年37歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州米德萊可坎布利其。

nameMeanings.name: Dean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English dene ‘valley’ (Old English denu), or a habitational name from any of several places in various parts of England named Dean or Dene from this word.

English: nickname or occupational name for the servant of a dean or nickname for someone thought to resemble a dean. A dean was an ecclesiastical official, the head of a chapter of canons or a church official with jurisdiction over a sub-division of an archdeaconry. Though no doubt some deans had illegitimate children, they were officially celibate, and in the main the surname is probably a nickname in origin, similar to Bishop , Prior , Priest , and Monk . The Middle English word deen, dien, dein, is a borrowing of Old French d(e)ien, doien from Latin decanus (originally a leader of ten men, from decem ‘ten’), and thus is a cognate of Deacon .
English: from the Middle English personal name Deyne (or Dene) a rhyming pet form of Reynald (see Reginald ).

variants.name: Bishop
meanings: English: from Middle English bissop, biscop, Old English bisc(e)op ‘bishop’, which comes via Latin from Greek episkopos ‘overseer’. The Greek word was adopted early in the Christian era as a title for an overseer of a local community of Christians, and has yielded cognates in every European language: French évêque, Italian vescovo, Spanish obispo, Russian yepiskop, German Bischof, etc. The word came to be applied as a surname for a variety of reasons, among them a supposed resemblance in bearing or appearance to a bishop, and selection as the ‘boy bishop’ on Saint Nicholas's Feast Day. In some instances the surname is from the rare Middle English (Old English) personal name Biscop ‘bishop’. As an Irish surname it is adopted for Mac Giolla Easpaig, meaning ‘servant of the bishop’ (see Gillespie ). In North America, this surname has absorbed, by assimilation and translation, at least some of continental European cognates, e.g. German Bischoff , Polish, Rusyn, Czech, and Slovak Biskup , Slovenian Škof (see Skoff ).

variants.name: Denman
meanings: English: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller in the valley’ from Middle English dene + man (see Dean 1).

variants.name: Deans
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Dean  .

variants.name: Denson
meanings: patronymic meaning ‘son of the dean’, see Dean 2. Since medieval deans were celibate, their sons would have to have been illegitimate, but le Dene was probably also used as a non-clerical nickname, so illegitimacy is not necessarily to be inferred.
patronymic meaning ‘son of Deyne or Dene’, a pet form of Reynald (Reginald); see Dean 3.
English:

variants.name: Deen
meanings: English: variant of   Dean  .
Dutch: ethnic name for a Dane, Middle Dutch Deen. Alternatively, from the personal name Deen, based on Old High German degan ‘squire, nobleman, thane’.

variants.name: Denton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places so called. The vast majority, including those in Cambridgeshire, Cumbria, Dumfries, Durham, Kent, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northumberland, Oxfordshire, Sussex, and Yorkshire are named from Old English denu ‘valley’ (see Dean 1) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. An isolated example in Northamptonshire appears in the Domesday Book as Dodintone meaning ‘enclosure, settlement associated with Dodda or Dudda’.

variants.name: Dann
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English dene ‘valley’ (Old English denu), which is often found as dane in placenames in Essex, Hertfordshire, Bedfordshire, Kent, and Sussex. Compare Dean .
English: perhaps from a pet form of the Middle English personal name   Daniel  .
German: topographic name for someone who lived by a pine tree, from Old High German tanna ‘pine tree’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Deaner
meanings: English (Worcestershire): topographic name for someone who lived in a valley, from Middle English dene ‘(thickly wooded) valley’ (see Dean 1) + the suffix -er, denoting an inhabitant.
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Diener  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Dean was born on 5 October 1622, in Dedham, Bucksport, Lincoln, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, her father, John Dean, was 26 and her mother, Susanna Fuller, was 17. She married Thomas Eames in 1640, in Dedham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 17 September 1660, in Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 37, and was buried in Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Dean est née le 5 octobre 1622, à Dedham, Bucksport, Lincoln, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, John Dean, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Susanna Fuller, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Eames en 1640, à Dedham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 17 septembre 1660, à Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 37 ans, et a été enterrée à Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Dean am 5. Oktober 1622 in Dedham, Bucksport, Lincoln, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Dean 26 und ihre Mutter Susanna Fuller 17. Sie hat 1640 in Dedham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Thomas Eames geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 17. September 1660 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Dean è nata il il 5 ottobre 1622 nel Dedham, Bucksport, Lincoln, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, sua padre, John Dean, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Susanna Fuller, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Eames nel 1640, nel Dedham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 17 settembre 1660, nel Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 37 anni, e fu sepolta a Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Deanが1622年10月5日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーリンコルンブクスプオルトデドハムで生まれた時，父John Deanは26歳，母Susanna Fullerは17歳であった。1640年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーデドハムでThomas Eamesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1660年9月17日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスフラミンガムで37歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ミッドレセックスカンブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Dean(이)가 1622년 10월 5일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 링코컨 벅스포트 데드함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Dean(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Susanna Fuller(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1640년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 데드함에서 Thomas Eames(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1660년 9월 17일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 프램잉함에서 37세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 미들레세스 캄브리지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Dean nasceu em 5 de outubro de 1622, em Dedham, Bucksport, Lincoln, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, John Dean, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Susanna Fuller, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Eames em 1640, em Dedham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 17 de setembro de 1660, em Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 37 anos, e foi sepultada em Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Dean родилась 5 октября 1622 г. в местности дедхам, буккспорт, линколн, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени John Dean было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Susanna Fuller, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Eames в 1640 г. в местности дедхам, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 17 сентября 1660 г. в местности фрамингхам, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 37 лет и была похоронена в местности камбридж, миддлесекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Dean nació el 5 de octubre de 1622, en Dedham, Bucksport, Lincoln, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, John Dean, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Susanna Fuller, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Thomas Eames en 1640, en Dedham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 17 de septiembre de 1660, en Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 37 años, y fue sepultada en Cambridge, Middlesex, Masachusets, Estados Unidos.
